summary: TokenForge backend full API test pass. 27/27 tests passed including LLM proxy round-trip via emergentintegrations, semantic cache hit on identical prompt, dashboard aggregations, Stripe checkout session creation, and admin RBAC. All MongoDB persistence verified.

minor.issue: Generic Exception raised as HTTP 502 with exception message in detail — could leak internal error info to clients. Consider sanitizing.
host_url derived from request.base_url may resolve to internal hostname behind ingress; webhook_url should be derived from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL or explicit env var for production.

Uses {$substr: ['$created_at', 0, 10]} treating created_at as string (ISO). Works because stored as ISO string, but inconsistent with idiomatic Mongo (date type + $dateToString). Functional but fragile.

action_items: Optional: derive Stripe webhook_url from env var rather than request.base_url for reliable production webhooks.
Optional: sanitize LLM exception messages before returning HTTP 502 detail.
Optional: store created_at as datetime (Mongo BSON date) and use $dateToString for timeseries grouping for robustness.
critical_code_review_comments: CORS allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False is fine here, but production should pin explicit origins.
ACCESS_TTL_MIN=60*24 → tokens valid for 24h with no refresh-token flow. Acceptable for MVP.
Semantic cache lookup scans latest 50 entries linearly — O(N*dim) per request, fine for MVP but won't scale; add a vector index later.
estimate_tokens is a heuristic (char/4 + words*1.33)/2 — diverges from tiktoken; billed pricing math is approximate.
/api/optimize is unauthenticated and accepts up to 50k chars — minor DOS risk; consider IP-based rate limit.
MODEL_PRICING_USD_PER_1K defaults to 0.002 for unknown models — silent fallback, could mislead cost-saved metrics.
Server file is ~780 lines; close to suggested 700-line split threshold. Consider splitting into routers (auth, proxy, billing, admin, dashboard).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@tokenforge.io / ForgeAdmin!2026 (loaded from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Test users created dynamically with TEST_qa_<run>@tokenforge.io / qaforge123.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates 2 unique test users (TEST_qa_<run>, TEST_qa2_<run>), 1 waitlist entry (TEST_wait_<run>), 3 proxy_requests, 1 cached prompt, 1 stripe checkout session in test mode. Admin user is seeded by backend startup hook.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL used = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (https://market-launch-43.preview.emergentagent.com). Note: the request mentioned a different preview URL but frontend/.env is the source of truth. Backend uses emergentintegrations real LLM calls — test prompts kept short to limit latency/cost. Semantic cache threshold is cosine>=0.98 with hashed-bag-of-words embedding (dim=256), so identical prompts reliably hit. /api/optimize is public; /api/proxy/chat requires X-TF-Key header; everything else uses Bearer JWT. Stripe is in test mode (sk_test_emergent), so billing/status returns 'pending' until manually paid via the returned URL; the 403 cross-user test is verified.
